import subprocess,re,sys
bins=['binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump','binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump']
base='gcc_stripped/'
# known bfd_malloc-like wrapper addrs found from malloc callers? We'll just find calls to malloc@plt and wrapper addresses manually? Use objdump full text and group funcs by endbr/prologue.
for b in bins:
    text=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-M','intel',base+b], text=True, errors='replace')
    lines=text.splitlines()
    # split by function-like endbr64 labels? stripped only labels for PLT and dynamic maybe not all funcs. Use endbr64 lines addresses as starts.
    # find candidates with add rax,0x1 then call an internal target then mov BYTE PTR [rax],0 pattern nearby.
    cand=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if 'add    rax,0x1' in l:
            win='\\n'.join(lines[i:i+45])
            if 'mov    rdi,rax' in win and 'call' in win and 'BYTE PTR' in win and ',0x0' in win:
                # find enclosing previous endbr or blank label
                start=None
                for j in range(i, max(-1,i-80), -1):
                    if 'endbr64' in lines[j]:
                        start=lines[j].split(':')[0].strip(); break
                cand.append((start, i+1, '\\n'.join(lines[max(0,i-15):i+45])))
    print('===',b,'candidates',len(cand))
    for start,ln,snip in cand[:30]:
        print('START',start,'line',ln)
        print(snip[:2500])
        print('---')

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re
bins={
'2.29':'gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29-o0-objdump',
'2.29.1':'gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump',
'2.30':'gcc_stripped/binutils-2.30-o0-objdump',
'2.31':'gcc_stripped/binutils-2.31-o0-objdump',
}
# find bfd_malloc wrapper: tiny function that calls malloc@plt and handles size 0; from known for first two, automatically by first function after call malloc? We'll use disassembly labels: wrappers are call targets frequently with malloc@plt in first 0x100 bytes.
for ver,path in bins.items():
    text=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-M','intel',path],text=True,errors='replace')
    lines=text.splitlines()
    # find functions by endbr64 line addresses
    starts=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        m=re.match(r'\\s*([0-9a-f]+):\\s+.*endbr64',l)
        if m: starts.append((int(m.group(1),16),i))
    funcs=[]
    for idx,(addr,i) in enumerate(starts):
        j=starts[idx+1][1] if idx+1<len(starts) else len(lines)
        funcs.append((addr,i,j))
    # detect malloc wrapper target: contains cmp [rbp-0x18],0; call malloc@plt; returns pointer; small <30 lines
    wrappers=[]
    for addr,i,j in funcs:
        body='\\n'.join(lines[i:j])
        if 'call' in body and '<malloc@plt>' in body and len(body.splitlines())<80:
            wrappers.append((addr, len(body.splitlines()), body[:500]))
    print('===',ver,'malloc-like wrappers',[(hex(a),n) for a,n,_ in wrappers[:8]])
    # pick one with only malloc not realloc
    malloc_addr=wrappers[0][0] if wrappers else None
    # Actually the bfd_malloc wrapper likely is first wrapper with malloc@plt and no other call besides failure; choose function with 'cmp QWORD PTR [rbp-0x18],0x0' and 'mov QWORD PTR [rbp-0x18],0x1'.
    for a,n,b in wrappers:
        if 'mov    QWORD PTR [rbp-0x18],0x1' in b or 'mov    QWORD PTR [rbp-0x18],0x1' in '\\n'.join(lines[[x[0] for x in funcs].index(a) if False else 0:0]):
            pass
    # use known pattern search via body full
    for addr,i,j in funcs:
        body='\\n'.join(lines[i:j])
        if '<malloc@plt>' in body and 'QWORD PTR [rbp-0x18],0x1' in body and len(body.splitlines())<60:
            malloc_addr=addr
            break
    print('malloc_addr',hex(malloc_addr) if malloc_addr else None)
    if not malloc_addr: continue
    callpat=f'call   {malloc_addr:x}'
    cands=[]
    for addr,i,j in funcs:
        body='\\n'.join(lines[i:j])
        if callpat in body:
            # find null write after add rax,rdx within function
            has_null='mov    BYTE PTR [rax],0x0' in body
            # find guard patterns near top
            top='\\n'.join(lines[i:i+50])
            if has_null:
                cands.append((addr,i,j,body.count(callpat),len(body.splitlines()),top))
    print('cands with call malloc wrapper and byte-null',len(cands))
    for addr,i,j,cnt,n,top in cands[:20]:
        print(hex(addr),'lines',n,'calls',cnt)
        print(top[:1800])
        print('---')
PY"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re
bins=['binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump','binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump']
base='gcc_stripped/'
for b in bins:
    text=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-M','intel',base+b], text=True, errors='replace')
    lines=text.splitlines()
    starts=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        m=re.match(r'\\s*([0-9a-f]+):\\s+.*endbr64',l)
        if m: starts.append((int(m.group(1),16),i))
    funcs=[]
    for idx,(addr,i) in enumerate(starts):
        j=starts[idx+1][1] if idx+1<len(starts) else len(lines)
        funcs.append((addr,i,j))
    print('\\n===',b)
    results=[]
    for addr,i,j in funcs:
        body=lines[i:j]
        textb='\\n'.join(body)
        # find stack slot storing rdx near top
        rdxslots=[]
        for k,l in enumerate(body[:25]):
            m=re.search(r'mov\\s+QWORD PTR \\[rbp-(0x[0-9a-f]+)\\],rdx',l)
            if m: rdxslots.append(m.group(1))
        if not rdxslots: continue
        if 'mov    BYTE PTR [rax],0x0' not in textb and 'mov    BYTE PTR [rdx],0x0' not in textb: continue
        # require early cmp/test on rdx slot
        score=0; evid=[]
        for slot in rdxslots:
            top='\\n'.join(body[:80])
            if f'QWORD PTR [rbp-{slot}],0x0' in top or f'QWORD PTR [rbp-{slot}]' in top and 'test' in top:
                score+=3; evid.append('early size ref '+slot)
            # add size+1 pattern
            if re.search(rf'mov\\s+rax,QWORD PTR \\[rbp-{slot}\\].{{0,120}}add\\s+rax,0x1', textb, re.S):
                score+=3; evid.append('size+1 '+slot)
            if re.search(rf'mov\\s+rdx,QWORD PTR \\[rbp-{slot}\\].{{0,100}}add\\s+rax,rdx', textb, re.S):
                score+=2; evid.append('buf[size] '+slot)
            if re.search(rf'mov\\s+rax,QWORD PTR \\[rbp-{slot}\\].{{0,120}}mov\\s+rsi,rax', textb, re.S):
                score+=1
        if score>=6:
            call_count=sum(1 for l in body if '\\tcall' in l or ' call ' in l)
            results.append((score,addr,j-i,call_count,evid,body[:120]))
    results=sorted(results, reverse=True)[:30]
    for score,addr,n,calls,evid,body in results:
        print(hex(addr),'score',score,'lines',n,'calls',calls,evid)
        print('\\n'.join(body[:70]))
        print('---')
PY"

import subprocess,re
bins=['binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31.1-o0-objdump']
for b in bins:
    path='gcc_stripped/'+b
    p=subprocess.run(['objdump','-d','-Mintel',path],capture_output=True,text=True,check=True)
    lines=p.stdout.splitlines()
    cand=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if 'mov' in l and 'QWORD PTR [rax+0x8]' in l:
            win=lines[i:i+8]
            s='\\n'.join(win)
            if 'movzx' in s and 'BYTE PTR [rax]' in s and ('cmp    al,0x3' in s or 'cmp    eax,0x3' in s):
                # check test between mov [rax+8] and movzx
                patched=any(('test   rax,rax' in x or 'cmp    rax,0x0' in x or 'cmp    QWORD PTR' in x and ',0x0' in x) for x in win)
                cand.append((i,patched,win))
    print('\\n###',b,'candidates',len(cand))
    for i,(idx,patched,win) in enumerate(cand[:50]):
        print('--- cand',i,'line',idx,'patched?',patched)
        print('\\n'.join(win))

text=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d',b], text=True, errors='ignore')
lines=text.splitlines()
qidx=[i for i,l in enumerate(lines) if 'call' in l and '<qsort@plt>' in l]
for i in qidx:
    # find preceding cmpq "'$0x0,-... within 120 lines and print concise
    addr=lines[i].strip().split('"':')[0]
    prev=[]
    for j in range(max(0,i-120),i):
        l=lines[j]
        if re.search(r'call\\s',l) or re.search(r'cmp[qbl]?\\s+\\"'$0x0,'"',l) or re.search(r'\\b(jle|jl|js|jns|jg|jge|je|jne)\\b',l):
            prev.append((j,l.strip()))
    print('\\nQsort at',addr)
    for j,l in prev[-25:]: print(l)
    print('--- qsort ctx ---')
    for l in lines[i-8:i+3]: print(l)

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re,sys
bins=['binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31.1-o0-objdump']
for b in bins:
    path='gcc_stripped/'+b
    frames=subprocess.check_output(['readelf','--debug-dump=frames',path],text=True,errors='ignore')
    fdes=[]
    for line in frames.splitlines():
        m=re.search(r'FDE .* pc=([0-9a-fA-F]+)\\.\\.([0-9a-fA-F]+)',line)
        if m:
            fdes.append((int(m.group(1),16),int(m.group(2),16)))
    dis=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d',path],text=True,errors='ignore')
    ins=[]
    for line in dis.splitlines():
        m=re.match(r'\\s*([0-9a-fA-F]+):\\s*(.*)',line)
        if m:
            ins.append((int(m.group(1),16),m.group(2)))
    # count calls per FDE; search for clusters with free calls and internal calls
    idx=0
    candidates=[]
    for start,end in fdes:
        while idx < len(ins) and ins[idx][0] < start: idx+=1
        j=idx
        lines=[]
        while j < len(ins) and ins[j][0] < end:
            lines.append(ins[j][1]); j+=1
        free_calls=sum('call' in x and 'free@plt' in x for x in lines)
        close_plt=sum('call' in x and ('close@plt' in x or 'fclose@plt' in x) for x in lines)
        total_calls=sum('\\tcall' in x or ' call' in x for x in lines)
        if free_calls>=6:
            candidates.append((start,end,free_calls,total_calls,close_plt))
    print('\\n',b,'FDEs',len(fdes),'candidates',len(candidates))
    for c in candidates[:60]:
        print('  %x..%x free=%d calls=%d closeplt=%d len=%d'% (c[0],c[1],c[2],c[3],c[4],c[1]-c[0]))
PY"

import subprocess,re
bins=['gcc_stripped/binutils-2.28-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.30-o0-objdump']
for b in bins:
    out=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-Mintel',b], text=True, errors='ignore')
    lines=out.splitlines()
    # collect windows that likely match asymbol scan loop: and 0x100 and and 0x2 near deref [tmp] and compares to section/offset
    hits=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines):
        if 'and    eax,0x100' in l or 'and    eax,0x2' in l:
            lo=max(0,i-80); hi=min(len(lines),i+80)
            w='\\n'.join(lines[lo:hi])
            score=0
            for pat in ['and    eax,0x100','and    eax,0x2','QWORD PTR [rax+0x20]','QWORD PTR [rax+0x10]','QWORD PTR [rbp-','mov    QWORD PTR [rbp-']:
                if pat in w: score += 1
            # exact loop-like deref: mov rax,[tmp]; mov rax,[rax]
            if w.count('QWORD PTR [rax]')>=4: score+=1
            if score>=5:
                # get center addr
                m=re.search(r'"'^'"\\s*([0-9a-f]+):', l)
                addr=m.group(1) if m else '?' 
                hits.append((int(addr,16) if addr"'!='"'?' else 0,i,score))
    # merge nearby hits
    merged=[]
    for addr,i,score in hits:
        if merged and i-merged[-1][2] < 120:
            merged[-1]=(merged[-1][0], addr, i, max(merged[-1][3],score))
        else:
            merged.append((addr,addr,i,score))
    print('\\n==',b,'hits',len(merged))
    for start,end,i,score in merged[:30]:
        print(hex(start),hex(end),'line',i,'score',score)

import subprocess,re,sys
bins=['gcc_stripped/binutils-2.28-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump','gcc_stripped/binutils-2.30-o0-objdump']
for b in bins:
    out=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-Mintel',b], text=True, errors='ignore')
    lines=[l for l in out.splitlines() if re.match(r'\\s*[0-9a-f]+:',l)]
    starts=[i for i,l in enumerate(lines) if 'endbr64' in l]
    starts.append(len(lines))
    cands=[]
    for a,bidx in zip(starts, starts[1:]):
        seg=lines[a:bidx]; text='\\n'.join(seg)
        # Pattern for _bfd_dwarf2_find_nearest_line's symbol search: compare symbol fields to abfd/section/offset + flags 0x100/0x2 and iterate pointer array by +8 until *tmp==0.
        score=0
        if 'and    eax,0x100' in text: score+=2
        if 'and    eax,0x2' in text: score+=2
        if re.search(r'cmp\\s+rdx,rax|cmp\\s+rax,rdx', text) and text.count('QWORD PTR [rbp-')>30: score+=1
        if text.count('QWORD PTR [rax+0x20]')>=2: score+=1
        if text.count('QWORD PTR [rax+0x10]')>=1: score+=1
        if text.count('add    QWORD PTR [rbp-')>=1: score+=1
        if re.search(r'add\\s+QWORD PTR \\[rbp-0x[0-9a-f]+\\],0x8', text): score+=2
        # incoming many args saved at positive rbp offsets? 
        if re.search(r'QWORD PTR \\[rbp\\+0x10\\]', text) and re.search(r'QWORD PTR \\[rbp\\+0x18\\]', text) and re.search(r'QWORD PTR \\[rbp\\+0x20\\]', text): score+=2
        if score>=9:
            start=int(re.match(r'\\s*([0-9a-f]+):',seg[0]).group(1),16)
            cands.append((start,score,len(seg),seg))
    print('\\n==',b,'cands',len(cands))
    for start,score,n,seg in cands[:20]:
        print('FUNC',hex(start),'score',score,'ninsn',n)
        # snippets around and 0x100/0x2 and add ptr
        idxs=[]
        for i,l in enumerate(seg):
            if 'and    eax,0x100' in l or 'and    eax,0x2' in l or re.search(r'add\\s+QWORD PTR \\[rbp-0x[0-9a-f]+\\],0x8',l):
                idxs.append(i)
        printed=set()
        for idx in idxs[:5]:
            lo=max(0,idx-12); hi=min(len(seg),idx+25)
            key=(lo,hi)
            if key in printed: continue
            printed.add(key)
            print('--- around',seg[idx].strip())
            for l in seg[lo:hi]: print(l)
PY"

b='gcc_stripped/binutils-2.28-o0-objdump'
out=subprocess.check_output(['objdump','-d','-Mintel',b], text=True, errors='ignore')
lines=[l for l in out.splitlines() if re.match(r'\\s*[0-9a-f]+:',l)]
starts=[i for i,l in enumerate(lines) if 'endbr64' in l]
starts.append(len(lines))
rows=[]
for a,bidx in zip(starts, starts[1:]):
    seg=lines[a:bidx]; text='\\n'.join(seg)
    c={}
    for pat in ['DWORD PTR [rax+0x18]','QWORD PTR [rax+0x20]','QWORD PTR [rax+0x10]','and    eax,0x100','and    eax,0x2','add    QWORD PTR [rbp-']:
        c[pat]=text.count(pat)
    loop_add=len(re.findall(r'add\\s+QWORD PTR \\[rbp-0x[0-9a-f]+\\],0x8',text))
    # incoming args pattern
    args=sum(1 for off in ['+0x10','+0x18','+0x20','+0x28','+0x30'] if f'QWORD PTR [rbp{off}]' in text)
    score=c['DWORD PTR [rax+0x18]']+c['QWORD PTR [rax+0x20]']+c['QWORD PTR [rax+0x10]']+3*c['and    eax,0x100']+2*c['and    eax,0x2']+2*loop_add+args
    if c['and    eax,0x100'] and c['and    eax,0x2'] and c['DWORD PTR [rax+0x18]'] and c['QWORD PTR [rax+0x20]'] and c['QWORD PTR [rax+0x10]']:
        start=int(re.match(r'\\s*([0-9a-f]+):',seg[0]).group(1),16)
        rows.append((score,start,len(seg),c,loop_add,args))
rows.sort(reverse=True)
for score,start,n,c,loop,args in rows[:80]:
    print(hex(start),'score',score,'n',n,'loopadd',loop,'args',args,c)
PY"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
from elftools.elf.elffile import ELFFile
from capstone import Cs, CS_ARCH_X86, CS_MODE_64
from capstone.x86 import *
from pathlib import Path

bins=['binutils-2.28-o0-objdump','binutils-2.29-o0-objdump','binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump','binutils-2.30-o0-objdump']
strings={
 'binutils-2.28-o0-objdump':[0x20820c,0x20821e,0x20824c,0x1ef0a0],
 'binutils-2.29-o0-objdump':[0x219a6c,0x219a7e,0x219aac,0x1fffe0],
 'binutils-2.29.1-o0-objdump':[0x21ac2c,0x21ac3e,0x21ac6c,0x201048],
 'binutils-2.30-o0-objdump':[0x21cc8c,0x21cc9e,0x21cccc,0x203388],
}
md=Cs(CS_ARCH_X86,CS_MODE_64); md.detail=True
for b in bins[:2]:
 p='gcc_stripped/'+b
 with open(p,'rb') as f:
  elf=ELFFile(f)
  text=elf.get_section_by_name('.text')
  data=text.data(); base=text['sh_addr']
  # fde ranges
  ranges=[]
  eh=elf.get_section_by_name('.eh_frame')
  if eh:
   for entry in eh.iter_CFI_entries():
    if entry['CIE_pointer']"'!=0:
     start=entry['"'initial_location']; end=start+entry['address_range']
     if base<=start<base+len(data): ranges.append((start,end))
  ranges.sort()
  def find_range(a):
   lo=0; hi=len(ranges)
   while lo<hi:
    m=(lo+hi)//2
    if ranges[m][0]<=a: lo=m+1
    else: hi=m
   i=lo-1
   if i>=0 and ranges[i][0]<=a<ranges[i][1]: return ranges[i]
   return None
  print('\\nBIN',b, 'fde count',len(ranges))
  hits=[]
  for ins in md.disasm(data,base):
   target=None
   # rip-relative mem
   for op in ins.operands:
    if op.type==X86_OP_MEM and op.mem.base==X86_REG_RIP:
     t=ins.address+ins.size+op.mem.disp
     if t in strings[b]: target=t
    if op.type==X86_OP_IMM and op.imm in strings[b]: target=op.imm
   if target:
    hits.append((ins.address,target,ins.mnemonic,ins.op_str,find_range(ins.address)))
  for h in hits:
   print('hit',hex(h[0]),'->',hex(h[1]),h[2],h[3],'range',tuple(hex(x) for x in h[4]) if h[4] else None)
PY"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess, re, struct, pathlib
files=['binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31.1-o0-objdump','binutils-2.32-o0-objdump','binutils-2.33.1-o0-objdump']
base=pathlib.Path('gcc_stripped')
# parse sections from readelf -SW
for name in files:
    path=base/name
    out=subprocess.check_output(['readelf','-SW',str(path)], text=True)
    secs={}
    for line in out.splitlines():
        m=re.match(r'\\s*\\[\\s*\\d+\\]\\s+(\\S+)\\s+\\S+\\s+([0-9a-fA-F]+)\\s+([0-9a-fA-F]+)\\s+([0-9a-fA-F]+)', line)
        if m:
            secs[m.group(1)]=tuple(int(x,16) for x in m.groups()[1:])
    data=path.read_bytes()
    # find strings offsets
    for s in [b\"Section '%s' has an invalid size: %#llx.\", b\"Can't get contents for section '%s'.\"]:
        idx=data.find(s)
        print(name, s[:20], 'offset/va', hex(idx) if idx>=0 else 'missing')
        if idx>=0:
            text_va,text_off,text_size=secs['.text']
            text=data[text_off:text_off+text_size]
            refs=[]
            for p in range(len(text)-4):
                disp=struct.unpack_from('<i', text, p)[0]
                if text_va+p+4+disp == idx:
                    refs.append(text_va+p) # disp field address
            print('  xrefs disp at', [hex(x) for x in refs[:20]], 'count', len(refs))
PY"

/bin/zsh -lc "python3 - <<'PY'
import subprocess,re,struct,pathlib
files=['binutils-2.30-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31-o0-objdump','binutils-2.31.1-o0-objdump','binutils-2.32-o0-objdump','binutils-2.33.1-o0-objdump']
base=pathlib.Path('gcc_stripped')
patterns=[b\"\\nSection '%s' has an invalid size: %#llx.\\n\", b\"\\nCan't get contents for section '%s'.\\n\"]
for name in files:
    path=base/name; data=path.read_bytes()
    out=subprocess.check_output(['readelf','-SW',str(path)], text=True)
    m=re.search(r'\\[\\s*16\\]\\s+\\.text\\s+\\S+\\s+([0-9a-f]+)\\s+([0-9a-f]+)\\s+([0-9a-f]+)', out)
    text_va,text_off,text_size=[int(x,16) for x in m.groups()]
    text=data[text_off:text_off+text_size]
    print('\\n',name,'text',hex(text_va),hex(text_off),hex(text_size))
    for s in patterns:
        idx=data.find(s)
        print(' pattern',s[:18], 'idx',hex(idx) if idx>=0 else 'missing')
        if idx<0: continue
        refs=[]
        for p in range(len(text)-4):
            disp=struct.unpack_from('<i', text, p)[0]
            if text_va+p+4+disp == idx:
                refs.append(text_va+p)
        print('  refs', [hex(r) for r in refs], 'count', len(refs))
PY"
